title: euphrasia nemorosa var. minima
intro: Euphrasia nemorosa var. minima, also known as small woodland eyebright, is a variant of the Euphrasia nemorosa plant species. This perennial herb is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Euphrasia nemorosa var. minima are attributed to its high content of glycosides, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, skin, and mucous membranes. In herbal preparations, Euphrasia nemorosa var. minima is often used in eye washes, salves, and teas to treat conditions such as conjunctivitis, hay fever, and minor wounds.
